description: My techs love this software. it is run with Windows startup and their preferences and watched IP's load quickly and efficiently. They give anything SolarWinds a great big fat thumbs up! I on the other hand only know the basics of it, and know it gives me exactly what I want in regards to IP Monitoring on my desktop. (mind you my tech had to set it up for me...)
author: Todd I.
pros: Very much a large and robust piece of software. Gives me a lot of abilities to manage my IP network.
cons: Too many settings, didn't seem to have a basic user interface as opposed to having an advanced only user interface. Too many options for the layman.

review_title: ipMonitor no longer an enterprise product
description: When it was unhindered by the 500 monitor limitation, this was the best NOC product I've used to date. It was easy to configure, easy to monitor and act upon monitor status changes, and it required little administrative overhead once initialized. Now, with the limitations discussed above, it's relegated to an entry-level product for startups and small businesses.
author: Jack E.
pros: The NOC UI is one of the most intuitive and actionable when configured correctly. Inexperienced NOC techs can quickly become proficient at responding to alerts or status changes with this system.
cons: The limitation on the number of monitors is problematic for all but the smaller end of the SMB market. I expect this change was implemented to drive customers to the Orion suite, which is much more costly and time-intensive to administer.

review_title: IPMonitor is a decent server monitoring solution
description: I used this software from about 2005-2016. It was effective, but could definitely use some UI changes as it didn't really change much at all in the time I was using it. It was really good and detailed when it came down to identifying problems and running reports, but could throw some false alarms from time-to-time. I would recommend this if the price is reasonable, but could also have a learning curve for new users that is not ideal.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: It was easy to keep track of server status' It had alarms you could set to go off if a server was having issues.
cons: The interface was dated and clunky to use. It would throw a lot of false alarms sometimes.

description: We have been using IP Monitor for the past 10+ years to monitor our IT Infrastructure (routers, switches, servers, etc). IP Monitor can be configured to generate alerts and a wide variety of reports
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: IPMonitor is extremely easy to set up and configure and is ideal for small companies that do not want to spend a lot of money for a network monitoring product
cons: The one feature I find that is missing is an easy method to purge out historic data that is no longer needed or valid.

description: ipMonitor's new management features make it easier to control and monitor large complex networks and to pinpoint issues in a timely manner. There's little to choose between ipMonitor and WhatsUp Gold for smaller networks, and either will do a good job. If you don't need WhatsUp Gold's scalability and ability to monitor distributed networks then ipMonitor is a good choice.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Ease of Installation and configuration Great Dashboards Great Reporting
cons: can take a lots of time to probe a huge network to discover the connected devices and configure the required monitors Cost is bit high in comparison with competitors Can monitor only 500 networked devices

review_title: IP Monitor - not so cost effective but meets the requirements in very highly basis
description: Using IP Monitor since long back as we are mid-size company easy visibility to network and devices very much helpful in reporting to clients through the acceptance process. It is been a valuable process and asset to our organization may be not so cost-effective but acceptable with its features.
author: shabbir S.
pros: we were looking for IP management of our network devices, servers, VMware hosts on a very high basis. we looking for agentless monitoring. many tools IP monitoring thinks Pinging is just enough but in reality, only administrators knew its importance. The visibility which it gives is really satisfying for us. at least it came to us at very affordable pricing so we were very happy about it.
cons: IP Monitor interface could be more effective, alerts could less dry, rebooting the IP monitor trigger every monitor we have sent an email. in the end, we need to submit it to managers reports which not so effective for us. cofiguration management.

description: ipMonitor has been a good option to monitor servers and networks, has great features, an amazing an useful interface. I small but significant tool is that you can configure the app on Windows so it automatically starts on Windows startup, I liked that.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: I can control my network devices, servers and hosts all in one console. The information is displayed in maps and diagrams, it's not all just text.
cons: Sometimes the "real time" notifications where sent 10 or 15 minutes after the incident, which was not really convenient.

author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Simple, easy to use software that does simple monitoring well.
cons: It is not being updated anymore and they really want you to switch to the higher priced orion suite. It is lacking on features and the ones it has are not well implemented or updated.

review_title: Quick and easy overview of Up/Down status
description: I started with IPMonitor, and quickly saw the business benefits coming from it, but later switched to the more comprehensive Network Monitor solution from SolarWinds which have much deeper reach into the network and server monitoring.
author: Bjarke V.
pros: What I like most id the ease of use with SolarWinds IP Monitor. It is SO easy to get up and running, and it will deliver results right away. It's super easy to add new devices to be monitored, and removed once they are no longer needed. There is also a free version to try out before committing to a purchase.
cons: The pre-build reports could be better, but with a little bit of extra effort, it's possible to create some pretty useful reports.

description: This program is great since it allows for thorough reporting and tracking. The ipmonitor support crew is extremely helpful and quick to respond. It's a Windows-compatible app. In other words, go for it!
author: Anil P.
pros: One of the greatest tools available for keeping tabs on your network's IP addresses is ipMonitor. Getting about and making use of it is a breeze. The ipMonitor interface is also quite well done. Features such as automated network detection and repair are also available.
cons: Scan times may sometimes be rather lengthy. If you don't need it for anything else, it's a fantastic monitoring tool.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Reliable monitoring tool without any significant development
description: If you can live with a feature set that was frozen around 6 years ago, this software will work fine for you. Don't count on the vendor adding any new features (IPv6, REST API, Mobile friendly display)
author: Markus O.
pros: Simple to use, broad list of monitors available, running stable for years
cons: There was no significant development during the last 5 years, but you have to keep paying the yearly maintenance renewal, otherwise the product will stop working. Release 10.8.2 was in 2013, then nothing happend until 10.8.3 was released in late 2018 with very minor changes.

review_title: Great entry tool for anysize network
description: It is not something I use every day however it is something that is used as a double check and also to help me manage the network.
author: Jeremy M.
pros: ease of use, the support around it and the way it integrates in to the other SolarWinds lineup of applications. Its very easy to understand and intuitive. I found that many of the features are exactly what I need to monitor the IP segments of my network.
cons: Honestly there is not much. The cost could be a bit lower. I would like to see them package it with Network Performance Monitor (NPM for short).

description: I love ipMonitor. We use it as our go to verify active ports, IP usage and auditing, and for planning network growth. Also nice to scan the environment for any devices we aren't expecting to see.
author: Chad H.
pros: Easy to scan your network to see which ports are active, nice repository for the whole company to utilize. Very customizable.
cons: I wish it had easier integration with DNS.

review_title: ipmonitor is a good solution but keep your options open
description: I've been able to monitor our infrastrucure with ipMonitor but now there are other options to consider.
author: Derek I.
pros: ipMonitor is very flexible and can monitor your whole infrastrucure.
cons: At times ipMonitor can be overwhelming to configure. There are lot's of competition for montioring software inclduing lot's of cloud options.

author: Stephan V.
pros: easy to use, no difficult steps to add a new device/server
cons: missing ipv6 support, submitted a request a few years ago but still no support... support costs based on no new features for the past years

description: monitoring network routers and switches. making sure utilization is within normal parameters.
author: Jason Y.
pros: you're able to customize the dashboard and set threshold alerts.
cons: sometimes it's hard to find what you're looking for.

author: ABDUL K.
pros: good for tracking user log on and log off's
cons: to complicated to access the consol, integraton of user ip is complicated

description: Great product to find available IP Address.
author: Aaron D.
pros: One of the best software/programs to monitor IP Address in your environment. Easy to use and navigate.
cons: The color scheme. Interface when adding a new subnet is kinda confusing at times.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months
